label: Transformer PPI (outcome)
mechanism: PPI for Electric Power & Specialty Transformer Manufacturing — the outcome.

label: Copper PPI
mechanism: Copper is the primary conductor for transformer windings; its input price feeds directly into finished transformer cost.

label: Aluminum mill shapes PPI
mechanism: Aluminum is the main alternative winding conductor and is used in housings and radiators.

label: Iron & steel PPI
mechanism: Electrical (silicon) steel forms the transformer core; broad iron & steel prices proxy that core-metal cost.

label: Iron & steel scrap PPI
mechanism: Scrap is the feedstock for electric-arc steelmaking; it sits upstream of finished steel and may lead it.

label: Global copper price
mechanism: Independent global (LME-based) copper benchmark; triangulates the domestic copper PPI.

label: Global aluminum price
mechanism: Independent global aluminum benchmark; triangulates the domestic aluminum PPI.

label: Global nickel price
mechanism: Nickel alloys electrical steels and stainless components; a global proxy for alloying-input cost.

label: WTI crude oil
mechanism: Energy is a major cost in smelting, rolling and transformer fabrication; crude oil proxies broad energy cost.

label: Electrical machinery & equip. PPI
mechanism: Broad electrical-goods price level; a sibling output market that may co-move with transformers.

label: Industrial production: utilities
mechanism: Utility output proxies grid-side demand; stronger grid activity can pull transformer demand and price.

label: Manufacturing employment
mechanism: General manufacturing labour demand; included as a negative-control anchor (weak expected link, per the brief).

label: Cold-rolled steel sheet (GOES proxy)
mechanism: Grain-oriented electrical steel — the transformer CORE material — is a specialised cold-rolled silicon-steel sheet. FRED has no GOES-specific series, so cold-rolled steel sheet & strip is the closest public PPI to the core input. Mechanistically the strongest single candidate.
note: Proxy for grain-oriented electrical steel (GOES); no exact GOES PPI exists on FRED.

label: Nonferrous wire & cable PPI
mechanism: The literal wound-conductor product (copper/aluminum wire & cable) that goes into windings — one step downstream of raw metal, one step upstream of the finished transformer.

label: Iron ores PPI
mechanism: Deepest upstream input to the steel core (ore -> steel -> electrical steel); may lead finished steel, and thus transformers, at the longest horizon.

label: Finished lubricants PPI
mechanism: Proxy for transformer insulating/mineral oil, a genuine dielectric-coolant input to oil-filled transformers; refined-oil cost distinct from crude.

label: Global zinc price
mechanism: Zinc galvanises steel transformer tanks, enclosures and structural steel; also a core member of the base-metals price cycle that feeds equipment cost.

label: Global lead price
mechanism: Lead is used in cable sheathing, some electrical components and counterweights, and triangulates the broad non-ferrous base-metals cycle.

label: Global tin price
mechanism: Tin is the solder that joins electrical connections in transformers, switchgear and assemblies; its price tracks connection/joint material cost.

label: Steel mill products PPI
mechanism: Broad finished steel (sheet, plate, structural) fabricated into transformer tanks, cores and mountings — one step downstream of raw iron & steel.

label: Steel wire PPI
mechanism: Steel wire is used for core banding, tie/support wire and structural bracing inside coils and assemblies.

label: Refined petroleum products PPI
mechanism: Transformer insulating/mineral oil is a refined-petroleum product; this also proxies broad energy and fabrication cost feeding equipment prices.

label: Plastic products PPI
mechanism: Plastics form insulation, bushings, coil formers and enclosures in transformers and switchgear; a genuine non-metal input.

label: Natural gas PPI
mechanism: Natural gas is a major energy input to metal smelting, rolling and component fabrication (and to grid gas generation); an upstream cost driver.

label: Electric power PPI
mechanism: Electricity is a dominant cost in aluminium and steel production and in fabrication; grid electricity price proxies that energy input.

label: Industrial production: manufacturing
mechanism: Broad manufacturing output cycle; rising activity pulls electrical-equipment orders and tightens input-material demand ahead of price moves.

label: Capacity utilization: manufacturing
mechanism: High utilisation signals supply tightness across manufacturing, which precedes upward pressure on equipment and input prices.

label: New orders: electrical equipment
mechanism: Manufacturers' new orders for electrical equipment, appliances & components — a direct forward-demand signal for the very goods transformers belong to; orders lead shipments and prices.

label: Construction spending: power
mechanism: Spending on power/grid construction directly pulls demand for transformers and grid equipment; the build cycle should lead equipment prices.

label: Employment: electrical-equip mfg
mechanism: Payrolls in electrical-equipment, appliance & component manufacturing proxy sector activity; hiring tracks the production/pricing cycle for this industry.

label: 10-Year Treasury yield
mechanism: Financing cost for capital-intensive grid and utility investment; rate moves lead the capex/build cycle that drives transformer demand. (Caveat: YoY of a yield is an unusual transform — interpret with care.)

label: Authorized in Permit-Issuing Places: Total U
mechanism: Construction/permit activity in the building pipeline; the build cycle leads electrical-equipment demand ahead of transformer orders and prices.
